content:
Announcing Software Should Work, an independent conference on software reliability on July 16-17 in Columbia, MO.

Software is usually buggy and slow, but it doesn't have to be. That's why I’m organizing a conference on the tools and techniques we use to make it correct and fast instead.

If you're excited about Roc, there's a very good chance you'll enjoy this conference too.
@Richard Feldman, @Ray Myers, @John Murray, @Andrew Kelley , and Hillel Wayne are all speaking. I'd love to have a strong contingent of Roc fans at SSW and really we already do. :smiley:

A huge thank you to RWX (@Dan Manges @Tommy Graves) and our other sponsors for helping make the conference possible.

Tickets are available now https://softwareshould.work
ssw_overview.png

That's why I’m organizing a conference

Wow, amazing, good luck @Isaac Van Doren!
Can we post this on our socials? CC @Richard Feldman
:+1: I'll just wait for Richard to sign off on that
"Isaac's Food Guide" :yum:

I went back through the Software Unscripted archives while I was laid up in the hospital. Many/most of these speakers are featured. I meant to take notes, but between physical difficulties and painkillers, not really an option. I dozed off a lot, a good thing :sweat_smile: Hopefully learned a lot through osmosis. But what an incredible smell software community we've uncovered!

"Isaac's Food Guide" :yum:

My favorite part of the website :smiley:
I posted on reddit, x and bluesky
:megaphone: The early bird discount ends this Friday!
Super excited to see all the Roc folks who have already bought tickets there :smile:
I'm thrilled to be on this lineup, great work Isaac!
I'm planning a talk on post-AI programming language design. Spoiler alert, Roc is well-designed :)
I'm debating it....not sure I want to deal with all the travel and what not though.
